name: pool-route
description: PreToolUse(Bash) pool auto-router, implemented in hexa-lang (`_pool_route.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). When the `pool` CLI's roster (`~/.pool/pool.json`) has ≥1 enabled host and a Bash command matches the heavy classifier (make · cargo · npm · gradle · pytest · go build · swift build · docker build · nvidia-smi · train · …) or a root-needing command, the command is rewritten via the hook `updatedInput` field to run on a pool host over ssh (`ssh <host> 'cd <workdir> && <cmd>'`) — transparent dispatch, not a suggestion. OS-capability filter (macOS-only / Linux-only commands restricted to matching-platform hosts) + round-robin load balance; autosync rsyncs the project to the host first. No roster / no classifier match / no eligible host → the command passes through untouched. NO opt-out by design — no env var, no config file, no exception list.
